content:
What is it with huge blocks of text today. I struggle to read it but I think I got it.

First questions are do you have an antivirus program and which is it?

That is the usual cause when you get told messages are downloading and then they just stop. Or you get the same few and it never ends, you just get the same start of the new list over and over. It is also a leading cause of issues about write privileges etc. Without being technical Thunderbird does not share well so if you have an antivirus scanning files on access (most windows users do) or a backup program trying to maintain a copy of the files in the web (be it some sort of streaming backup or a modified storage location as the default ones are not usually included in web backups)

Disabling the scanning of mail is sometime all that is required. But the more comprehensive the third party antivirus the more likely it is to mess things in the nest. Note that you can uninstall third part antivirus products and windows defender will just step in to cover you security wise. So do not be afraid of removing the antivirus to see if that is the issue.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Do you need them physically in one inbox, or presented via thunderbird as one virtual inbox. The virtual inbox is an easy setup and lets you treat all mail from one virtual inbox and, if desired, all sent messages in one virtual sent folder. Let me know.

On your other approach, if a gmail account is forwarding to a second account, why have the gmail account in thunderbird at all? Forward it from the gmail online account.

content:
peter197 said
I keep entering my details but it does not work, I have sent the tested the settings and they are OK nbut the system keeps asking me to re enter password .I have not changed any passwords and only want a pop3 account to send and receive <peter@hainsworthsboatyard.co.uk> I have my own server and domain name through Maxer via C panel .I use microsoft outlook OK but cannot alter my e mail box size

I have to ask. Is your account in outlook configured as IMAP or POP? I think we need to look at the basic original issue before moving on to Thunderbird and it's issues. I think they may be related, but it is only a gut feeling.

So check the account in cpanel. How much quota is available there? See https://my.maxer.com/knowledgebase/60/How-to-set-the-storage-space-limit-of-an-email-account-or-mailbox.html

It does sounds to me like the cpanel quota is exhausted and your outlook is bucking about it and Thunderbird can not create an account for you because the server is refusing to play along because the account has a full quota situation. It would certainly explain the issues in Outlook. It is a stretch for Thunderbird. Quota is a server side setting so neither outlook or Thunderbird would be able to modify it.

.
creator: MattAuSupport
